voice_agent_deployment_and_examples.overview: Complete guide for deploying voice-enabled agents with real-world examples, deployment automation, credential management, and troubleshooting scenarios.

principle.name: security_first
principle.description: Never expose credentials in code or version control

practices.practice: Use environment variables for sensitive data
Provide sample credential files with placeholders
Use connection objects in watsonx Orchestrate

principle.name: automation
principle.description: Automate deployment for consistency and repeatability

principle.name: validation
principle.description: Verify deployment success before going live

file.name: credentials.sample.yaml
file.content:
# Voice Agent Credentials Template # Copy to credentials.yaml and fill in actual values # DO NOT commit credentials.yaml to version control
# Watson Services watson: stt: api_key: "YOUR_WATSON_STT_API_KEY" service_url: "https://api.us-south.speech-to-text.watson.cloud.ibm.com" tts: api_key: "YOUR_WATSON_TTS_API_KEY" service_url: "https://api.us-south.text-to-speech.watson.cloud.ibm.com"
# watsonx Orchestrate orchestrate: api_key: "YOUR_ORCHESTRATE_API_KEY" url: "https://your-instance.watson-orchestrate.ibm.com" space_id: "YOUR_SPACE_ID"
# Channel Integrations channels: genesys: api_key: "YOUR_GENESYS_API_KEY" client_secret: "YOUR_GENESYS_CLIENT_SECRET" twilio: account_sid: "YOUR_TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID" auth_token: "YOUR_TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN" whatsapp_number: "whatsapp:+14155238886" slack: bot_token: "xoxb-YOUR-BOT-TOKEN" signing_secret: "YOUR_SIGNING_SECRET"

file.name: .env.sample
file.content:
# Environment Variables Template # Copy to .env and fill in actual values
WATSON_STT_API_KEY=your_stt_api_key WATSON_STT_URL=https://api.us-south.speech-to-text.watson.cloud.ibm.com WATSON_TTS_API_KEY=your_tts_api_key WATSON_TTS_URL=https://api.us-south.text-to-speech.watson.cloud.ibm.com
WXO_API_KEY=your_orchestrate_api_key WXO_URL=https://your-instance.watson-orchestrate.ibm.com WXO_SPACE_ID=your_space_id
ENVIRONMENT=development LOG_LEVEL=INFO

sections.section: How to get Watson STT/TTS credentials
How to get watsonx Orchestrate API key

file.name: deployment_plan.md
file.template:
# Voice Agent Deployment Plan
## Overview **Project:** [Project Name] **Deployment Date:** [Date] **Environment:** [Development/Staging/Production]
### Objectives - Deploy voice-enabled agent to watsonx Orchestrate - Configure voice channels - Set up monitoring and alerting - Validate end-to-end functionality
### Components - Agent: [Agent Name] - Voice Config: [Config Name] - Channels: [List of channels] - Integrations: [External systems]
## Pre-Deployment Checklist
### Environment Preparation - [ ] watsonx Orchestrate instance accessible - [ ] Required credentials obtained and validated - [ ] Network connectivity verified - [ ] Backup of current configuration completed
### Credential Setup - [ ] credentials.yaml populated with actual values - [ ] Environment variables configured - [ ] Orchestrate connections created - [ ] Channel provider accounts configured
### Testing - [ ] Agent tested in development environment - [ ] Voice configuration validated - [ ] Channel integrations tested - [ ] Security review passed
## Deployment Steps
### Step 1: Deploy Agent ```bash ./deployment.sh deploy-agent ``` **Expected Output:** Agent created/updated successfully **Validation:** Verify agent appears in agent list
### Step 2: Deploy Voice Configuration ```bash ./deployment.sh deploy-voice-config ``` **Expected Output:** Voice config imported successfully **Validation:** Test STT/TTS functionality
### Step 3: Configure Channels ```bash ./deployment.sh deploy-channels ``` **Expected Output:** Channels created and configured **Validation:** Verify webhook URLs are accessible
### Step 4: Deploy Tools ```bash ./deployment.sh deploy-tools ``` **Expected Output:** All tools imported successfully **Validation:** Verify tools are accessible to agent
## Post-Deployment Validation
### Health Checks - [ ] Agent responds to test messages - [ ] Voice input/output working correctly - [ ] All channels operational - [ ] Tool invocations successful
### Smoke Tests 1. Basic interaction test through each channel 2. Voice quality test (STT accuracy, TTS naturalness) 3. Integration test (external API calls) 4. Load test (if applicable)
### Monitoring Setup - [ ] Logging configured - [ ] Metrics collection enabled - [ ] Alerts configured - [ ] Dashboard created
## Rollback Procedure
### Rollback Triggers - Critical functionality broken - Performance degradation > 50% - Security vulnerability discovered
### Rollback Steps 1. Execute rollback script: `./deployment.sh rollback` 2. Verify previous version functionality 3. Notify stakeholders 4. Schedule post-mortem
## Troubleshooting
### Common Issues **Agent not responding:** Check agent status, verify credentials **Voice quality problems:** Verify STT/TTS configuration **Channel failures:** Verify webhook URLs and authentication
### Contact Information - Deployment Lead: [Name] - [Email] - Technical Support: [Team] - [Email]

file.name: deployment.sh

example.name: customer_service_voice_agent
example.title: Building Customer Service Voice Agent from Scratch
example.scenario: Build voice-enabled customer service agent handling order inquiries, returns, and product questions via phone and WhatsApp.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of this voice agent?
Which channels do you need?
What key capabilities should it have?
What external systems will it integrate with?
What languages should the agent support?
STT/TTS provider preferences?
Audio quality requirements?
Expected call volume?

step.action: Present plan for user review and approval

key_learnings.learning: Always start with comprehensive requirements gathering
Search ADK documentation at critical steps
Use sample credential files, never actual credentials
Test voice quality early and often

example.name: migrating_text_to_voice
example.title: Migrating Existing Text Agent to Voice
example.scenario: User has existing text-based support agent and wants to add voice capabilities via phone channel.

findings.finding: Responses include bullet points and formatting

changes.change: Remove visual formatting, use natural speech

step.recommendation: Create new agent for cleaner separation

step.action: Update agent instructions with voice guidelines

key_learnings.learning: Text agents need significant optimization for voice
Response length is critical for voice interactions
Confirmation steps are more important in voice
Visual formatting doesn't translate to voice

example.name: multi_language_voice_agent
example.title: Building Multi-Language Voice Agent
example.scenario: Voice agent supporting English and Spanish for customer service

recommended_approach.choice: Separate voice configs for better control

implementation.step: Configure agent to use appropriate config based on language

example.agent_instructions_addition: ## Language Handling - Detect user's language from first utterance - Switch to appropriate language for responses - Maintain language consistency throughout conversation - Offer language switch option if needed

implementation.step: Use environment variables in deployment script

diagnosis.check: Verify audio quality and sample rate

solution.step: Switch to appropriate acoustic model (telephony vs broadband)
Consider custom language model for domain terms

example.name: high_latency
example.problem: Noticeable delay between user speech and agent response

solution.step: Optimize agent instructions for faster processing
Use regional endpoints closer to users

example.name: channel_integration_failure
example.problem: Webhook not receiving events from channel provider

diagnosis.check: Verify webhook URL is correct and accessible

solution.step: Test webhook URL with curl or Postman
Verify credentials are correct and not expired
Check webhook configuration in provider console

practice.guideline: Search ADK documentation at critical steps
Never ask for or store actual credentials
Test with diverse voices and accents
